intro: While you're here, we hope you take time to enjoy the beauty, flavors, and charm of our city. If you need help planning tours or activities, reach out to our wedding planning team.

items.name: Andián Bistro & Café
items.note: A French-inspired bistro in the heart of San Benito, known for excellent coffee, fresh brunch plates, and a downstairs case full of pastries and chocolates.

items.name: Alquimia Coffee
items.note: A sleek 'coffee lab' pouring 100% Salvadoran beans, recently ranked the #3 coffee shop in the world; come for a cup, stay for the sensory experience.

items.name: El Mirador de la Giralda
items.note: A mountain-ridge restaurant about 40 minutes above the city in Comasagua, with panoramic volcano views, Swiss-Salvadoran cooking, and live music.

items.name: El Xolo
items.note: Set inside the National Anthropology Museum, this is El Salvador's most celebrated restaurant (Latin America's 50 Best), reinventing native corn and local ingredients.

items.name: Brutto
items.note: A stylish rooftop spot with skyline views, serving modern Asian-influenced plates, standout sushi, and inventive cocktails, all designed to share.

items.name: Al Pomodoro
items.note: A San Benito institution for authentic Italian: handmade pastas, wood-fired pizzas, and a deep wine list in a warm, family-friendly setting that welcomes all ages.

items.name: Ataco/Apaneca
items.note: Two colorful mountain towns along the Ruta de las Flores, famous for muraled streets, coffee farms, waterfalls, and cool highland air.
items.whereToEat: Piccolo Giardino — a charming garden-set Italian spot in Ataco known for wood-fired pizzas and fresh pasta.

items.name: Surf City / La Libertad
items.note: El Salvador's Pacific surf coast, with black-sand beaches like El Tunco and El Zonte, world-class waves, sunsets, and fresh seafood.
items.whereToEat: Beto's — a coastal institution since 1986, serving fresh seafood on a cliff terrace right over the ocean.

items.name: Cerro Verde
items.note: A national park in the cloud forest, home to the hike up the Santa Ana volcano (Ilamatepec) and sweeping views of its turquoise crater lake.
items.whereToEat: Casa 1800 — a boutique lodge and restaurant perched in the park, with Salvadoran plates and floor-to-ceiling volcano views.
